title: GLBA §314.4(c)(1) — Access controls: customer-information access governance and least-privilege enforcement
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=GLBA, clause=§314.4(c)(1), available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §314.4(c)(1).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GLBA §314.4(c)(1) (Access controls) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.38: GLBA §314.4(c)(1) — Access controls: customer-information access governance and least-privilege enforcement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.38' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: IAM access logs, privilege-escalation events, access-review campaign results, data-classification tags.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=iam sourcetype IN (access_reviews, privileged_sessions) earliest=-30d
| eval is_review=if(sourcetype="access_reviews", 1, 0)
| eval is_session=if(sourcetype="privileged_sessions", 1, 0)
| eval review_completed=if(is_review=1 AND review_status="completed", 1, 0)
| eval privileged_access=if(is_session=1 AND privilege_level IN ("admin", "root", "elevated"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(is_review) AS total_reviews sum(review_completed) AS completed_reviews sum(privileged_access) AS priv_sessions dc(user) AS distinct_users BY business_unit
| eval review_completion_pct=if(total_reviews>0, round(100*completed_reviews/total_reviews, 1), 0)
| eval access_risk=case(review_completion_pct<80, "high", review_completion_pct<95, "medium", true(), "low")
| where access_risk!="low"
| table business_unit, total_reviews, completed_reviews, review_completion_pct, priv_sessions, distinct_users, access_risk
description: Monitors access review completion rates and privileged session activity for GLBA §314.4(c)(1) access controls. Separates periodic access certification from per-session monitoring, fixing the logic that previously confused individual access events with periodic reviews.
value: §314.4(c)(1) requires implementing access controls to limit access to customer information to authorised users. Continuous monitoring of privileged access and review completeness enforces least-privilege.
implementation: (1) Tag systems holding customer information with system_tag=glba_scope; (2) ingest IAM access and privilege-change logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate excessive privileged access. Indexes required: `index=iam`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=access_log`, `sourcetype=privilege_changes`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `access_reviewed`, `department`, `distinct_users`, `privileged_access`, `privileged_count`, `privileged_pct`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IAM access logs, privilege-escalation events, access-review campaign results, data-classification tags.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag systems holding customer information with system_tag=glba_scope; (2) ingest IAM access and privilege-change logs; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate excessive privileged access.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iam sourcetype IN (access_reviews, privileged_sessions) earliest=-30d
| eval is_review=if(sourcetype="access_reviews", 1, 0)
| eval is_session=if(sourcetype="privileged_sessions", 1, 0)
| eval review_completed=if(is_review=1 AND review_status="completed", 1, 0)
| eval privileged_access=if(is_session=1 AND privilege_level IN ("admin", "root", "elevated"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(is_review) AS total_reviews sum(review_completed) AS completed_reviews sum(privileged_access) AS priv_sessions dc(user) AS distinct_users BY business_unit
| eval review_completion_pct=if(total_reviews>0, round(100*completed_reviews/total_reviews, 1), 0)
| eval access_risk=case(review_completion_pct<80, "high", review_completion_pct<95, "medium", true(), "low")
| where access_risk!="low"
| table business_unit, total_reviews, completed_reviews, review_completion_pct, priv_sessions, distinct_users, access_risk
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
